raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.3
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Your request is broad. Should I prioritize a scientific soundness check (focusing on the core claims, logic, and evidence) or a writing and presentation review (focusing on clarity, structure, and polish)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The request asks for a general opinion, which requires understanding and evaluating the core contribution claim of the MolPanel framework.",
    "R1.2": "A general assessment requires checking the logical flow from the premise of ensemble evaluation to the conclusion of improved hit rates.",
    "R1.3": "The core of any scientific opinion is whether the reported evidence, such as the virtual screen results, robustly supports the central claim of enrichment.",
    "R1.4": "To form an opinion, I must assess the novelty of the panel-of-models approach against the backdrop of existing virtual screening techniques.",
    "RX.1": "My assessment must consider whether the abstract appropriately discloses uncertainties, such as the performance on targets beyond the three benchmarks.",
    "RX.2": "A key part of 'what I think' involves checking if the claims about 'substantial improvement' and general applicability are overstated.",
    "RX.3": "I must ensure my evaluation respects the stated scope, which is a post-hoc filter for generative models, not an end-to-end discovery method.",
    "RX.4": "My opinion should consider any potential downstream harms, such as the risk of the panel systematically filtering out unconventional but effective drug candidates.",
    "RX.5": "I must operate on the provided text, noting that claims about public data sources are taken at face value without external verification."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Your request is broad. Should I prioritize a scientific soundness check (focusing on the core claims, logic, and evidence) or a writing and presentation review (focusing on clarity, structure, and polish)?

rationale.R1.1: The request asks for a general opinion, which requires understanding and evaluating the core contribution claim of the MolPanel framework.
rationale.R1.2: A general assessment requires checking the logical flow from the premise of ensemble evaluation to the conclusion of improved hit rates.
rationale.R1.3: The core of any scientific opinion is whether the reported evidence, such as the virtual screen results, robustly supports the central claim of enrichment.
rationale.R1.4: To form an opinion, I must assess the novelty of the panel-of-models approach against the backdrop of existing virtual screening techniques.
rationale.RX.1: My assessment must consider whether the abstract appropriately discloses uncertainties, such as the performance on targets beyond the three benchmarks.
rationale.RX.2: A key part of 'what I think' involves checking if the claims about 'substantial improvement' and general applicability are overstated.
rationale.RX.3: I must ensure my evaluation respects the stated scope, which is a post-hoc filter for generative models, not an end-to-end discovery method.
rationale.RX.4: My opinion should consider any potential downstream harms, such as the risk of the panel systematically filtering out unconventional but effective drug candidates.
rationale.RX.5: I must operate on the provided text, noting that claims about public data sources are taken at face value without external verification.
